scenario.name: skill_not_found
scenario.trigger: No planning skill matches the identified planning system (e.g., user says "Jira" but no prd-jira-planning skill exists).

required_actions.action: Report the gap explicitly: state which system was identified and which skill is missing.
Offer two options: (a) proceed with local-only planning without system synchronization, or (b) create the missing skill first using skill-writer mode.
If user chooses local-only, mark all artifacts as PROVISIONAL - NOT SYNCED TO [SYSTEM].

prohibited_actions.action: Do not attempt to use a different skill as a substitute without telling the user.
Do not claim system synchronization when no skill was loaded.

scenario.name: system_unavailable
scenario.trigger: The planning system is unavailable (MCP tools inaccessible, API error, authentication failure).

required_actions.action: Stop all system write operations immediately.
Report the blocker clearly: which system, which operation failed, what the impact is.
Continue only with provisional local planning draft if user approves temporary offline drafting.
Mark all provisional artifacts as PROVISIONAL - NOT SYNCED TO [SYSTEM].

prohibited_actions.action: Do not claim synchronization to the planning system when writes did not occur.

scenario.name: stale_or_conflicting_state
scenario.trigger: Planning system state appears outdated, contradictory, or incomplete relative to expectations.

required_actions.action: Re-fetch current state before proposing additional writes.
Identify and document conflicts and the proposed resolution path.
Ask one focused clarification question when conflict cannot be resolved from available state.

scenario.name: validation_gate_override
scenario.trigger: User wants to proceed to decomposition despite one or more PRD validation dimensions being "low" or "medium".

required_actions.action: Do not silently skip the gate.
For each non-high dimension, state the specific risk that is being accepted.
Require the user to explicitly acknowledge each risk in writing before proceeding.
Note the overridden dimensions in the PRD and in the coordinator handoff summary.

prohibited_actions.action: Do not proceed without explicit per-dimension risk acknowledgment.
Do not downgrade the severity of unresolved validation gaps to justify moving forward.

scenario.name: research_dispatch_failure
scenario.trigger: A dispatched project-research agent fails, returns inconclusive results, or is blocked.

required_actions.action: Report the research failure to the user with the specific question that was unresolvable.
Ask the user if they have domain knowledge that can fill the gap.
If the gap is in technical feasibility (validation dimension 6), mark that dimension as "medium" with the unresolved question documented.
Do not fabricate an answer to fill the research gap.

scenario.name: overbroad_hld_issue_scope
scenario.trigger: An HLD Issue draft is too broad for one focused architect-led execution cycle.

required_actions.action: Split into smaller HLD Issues with explicit scope boundaries and dependency order.
Add testable acceptance criteria for each resulting issue.
Verify each resulting issue traces back to the parent User Story Project and PRD Initiative.

scenario.name: handoff_not_ready
scenario.trigger: Artifacts are incomplete, validation gate was not cleared, or required fields are missing for execution.

required_actions.action: Do not hand off to coordinator yet.
Identify the specific blocker: which artifact is missing what field, or which validation dimension is not "high".
Complete the missing content or resolve the validation gap before handoff.

prohibited_actions.action: Do not hand off to coordinator with unanswered validation gaps or incomplete artifacts.
